title: Opisthopappus taihangensis
intro: Opisthopappus taihangensis is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Opisthopappus taihangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Opisthopappus taihangensis include decoctions, infusions, and granules, which are used to treat a range of health issues in traditional Chinese medicine.
